packet.task_claim: A candidate discovery task should be admitted only when it decomposes an open-ended question into measurable variables, exploratory steps, validation, and falsification.

measurable_variables.name: hypothesis_count
measurable_variables.measurement_method: count atomic hypotheses generated from one task packet

measurable_variables.name: unsupported_promotion_rate
measurable_variables.measurement_method: fraction of promoted claims lacking validation evidence

hypothesis_decomposition: Separate the open-ended task into candidate mechanisms rather than a single answer.
Map each mechanism to at least one measurable variable and one failure condition.
exploratory_steps: Generate a public-safe synthetic table with timestamped observations and source labels.
Run the task through the validation plan before any discovery-memory promotion.
packet.validation_plan: Accept the task only if variables, exploratory steps, temporal boundary, and falsification route are all present and privacy-safe.
packet.falsification_route: Reject or revise this gate if open-ended scientific tasks can be promoted safely without decomposition, measurable variables, and validation boundaries.
packet.revision_reason: Frontier scientific AI benchmarks increasingly test workflow realism and open-ended exploratory reasoning rather than answer correctness alone.
packet.next_executable_action: Run validator against all synthetic fixtures and wire gate before discovery-memory admission.

packet.task_claim: A task packet can use raw private longitudinal traces for exploratory discovery.
hypothesis_decomposition: Look for patterns in private data.
packet.revision_reason: Negative fixture for privacy boundary enforcement.

packet.task_claim: A generated answer should be accepted as a discovery task without decomposing its assumptions.
packet.falsification_route: Reject if citations are obviously wrong.
packet.next_executable_action: Reject this packet until decomposition exists.
